what_it_does: Introduces a feature-by-feature comparison of Obsidian qualities against the project's own flat, id-keyed .md ledger format, stating scope, a Clean/Partial/None rating scheme, and a mobile/non-developer lens; the excerpt contains only the framing/scope paragraph and no actual feature ratings.
observations: YAML frontmatter declares type=block, source=claude_code, state=current, channel=dragnet-development, and a part_of link to board://item-3480c157.
Title marks priority P1 and frames a feature-by-feature, grounded comparison with Obsidian.
Body asserts Obsidian is treated only as a HOST/LENS while the project's fabric remains canonical per OBSIDIAN-HOST-QUESTION.md.
Proposed rating labels are Clean, Partial, and None, each judged by value to a non-developer on a phone.
Author explicitly refuses to converge on a single answer, noting a wide spread and that the best option for Tim is the most gated.
File content stops after the scope statement; no named Obsidian features or ratings are present.
standouts: Explicit non-convergence and admission of a wide spread of findings.
User-centric evaluation lens: value to a non-developer on a phone.
Canonical-fabric guardrail: Obsidian is host/lens, not source of truth.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, timestamps, history, and typed links.
Follows board:// address scheme and part_of edge semantics.
Markdown body with a priority prefix (P1) in the title.
References an external decision document (OBSIDIAN-HOST-QUESTION.md) for scope authority.
concerns: The file promises a feature-by-feature mapping but contains only the framing paragraph.
Key terms (Clean/Partial/None, 'Tim', 'most gated') are undefined in this file.
No actual Obsidian features are named or rated, so the item is currently a stub/preamble.
notes: A priority-1 noticeboard item that sets up a structured Obsidian-vs-ledger evaluation but has not yet delivered the feature-by-feature analysis.
questions: Which specific Obsidian features are being evaluated?
What are the precise definitions of Clean, Partial, and None?
Who is Tim and what is the 'most gated' best thing?
Where is OBSIDIAN-HOST-QUESTION.md and what does it mandate?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a feature-by-feature grounded mapping; actual content is only the scope/methodology preamble, so it is a partial/stub.
intention_signals: To create a disciplined, user-centered framework for deciding which Obsidian capabilities can be adopted without letting Obsidian become the canonical source of truth.
novelty: Treats Obsidian as a replaceable host/lens while preserving a custom flat-file ledger with typed address-edges, and explicitly refuses to collapse a wide evaluation into a single recommendation.
contribution: Provides evaluation framing, a mobile/non-developer lens, and canonical-fabric guardrails that a fused system could reuse when comparing external tool features to the internal ledger format.
summary_for_embedding: A priority-1 noticeboard item that frames a feature-by-feature evaluation of Obsidian against the project's flat id-keyed .md ledger, using Clean/Partial/None ratings judged by non-developer phone usability, but contains only the scope statement.
intention_for_embedding: To establish a structured, non-convergent comparison method for deciding which Obsidian qualities can be adopted while keeping the project's fabric canonical.
suggested_fields: evaluation_status to capture whether an item is framing, in-progress, or complete
rating_scale to record definitions of Clean/Partial/None
target_persona to name the user lens (e.g., Tim / non-developer on phone)
gating_reason to explain why a recommended option is blocked
proposed_edge_kinds: references_document for links to scope documents like OBSIDIAN-HOST-QUESTION.md
evaluates_feature for a block that assesses an external tool feature
scoped_by for a block whose scope is governed by another item/document
